## Before You Start — One-Time Label Setup

> Skip this section if labels are already configured in the repository.

Go to: **https://github.com/phatnguyen975/eshop-sut → Settings → Labels → New label**

Create the following labels if they do not exist:

| Label                | Color     | Purpose                                 |
| -------------------- | --------- | --------------------------------------- |
| `bug`                | `#d73a4a` | All defect reports                      |
| `severity: fatal`    | `#b60205` | System crash / data loss                |
| `severity: serious`  | `#e4e669` | Core feature broken / security violated |
| `severity: medium`   | `#0075ca` | SRS deviation, feature still works      |
| `severity: cosmetic` | `#cfd3d7` | Minor UI issues only                    |
| `FR-01`              | `#5319e7` | Account Registration                    |
| `FR-07`              | `#1d76db` | Shopping Cart                           |
| `FR-17`              | `#0e8a16` | Coupon Management                       |
| `FR-03`              | `#fbca04` | Forgot Password                         |
| `security`           | `#b60205` | SEC-xx violations                       |
| `api`                | `#0052cc` | API-level bugs                          |
| `ui`                 | `#e4e669` | UI / frontend bugs                      |
| `dom`                | `#fbca04` | DOM / HTML structure bugs               |

## How to Use This Guide

1. Open: **https://github.com/phatnguyen975/eshop-sut/issues/new**
2. For each issue below:  
   a. Copy the **Title** exactly as shown into the Title field  
   b. Paste the **Body** content into the description area  
   c. Add the **Labels** listed (select from the dropdown)  
   d. If a screenshot is listed in the Evidence section: drag and drop the file into the body text area — GitHub will upload and embed it automatically  
   e. Click **Submit new issue**  
   f. Note the issue number from the URL (e.g., the URL becomes `.../issues/15` → number is `15`)
3. After posting ALL issues for this FR, return and type:
   ```
   Issue numbers for FR-03: BUG-001=#X, BUG-002=#Y, ...
   ```
   The agent will update `qa-artifacts/bug-reports/FR03-bugs.md` with these numbers.

## Issue 1 of 6

### BUG-001 — Reset Password API stores passwords in plaintext

**Title:**

```text
[BUG][FR03] Reset Password API stores passwords in plaintext
```

**Labels:** `bug`, `severity: serious`, `FR-03`, `api`, `security`

**Screenshot to attach:** None — API test, no screenshot required

**Body:**

````markdown
## Summary

Reset Password API stores passwords in plaintext instead of bcrypt hash when successfully resetting password (per SEC-01)

## Bug Details

| Field          | Value       |
| -------------- | ----------- |
| **Bug ID**     | BUG-001     |
| **Linked TCs** | FR03-EP-001 |
| **Channel**    | State / DB  |
| **Severity**   | Serious     |
| **Priority**   | High        |

## Environment

| Component    | Value                 |
| ------------ | --------------------- |
| OS / Device  | Windows 11            |
| Runtime Env  | Node.js 24.x          |
| Backend      | http://localhost:3000 |
| Frontend/App | N/A (API-only test)   |
| Test Account | test@eshop.com        |

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Ensure the EShop backend is running at http://localhost:3000.
2. Send the following request to fetch OTP:
   ```bash
   curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/forgot-password \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"email":"test@eshop.com"}'
   ```
3. Send the following request to reset password using the fetched resetToken:
   ```bash
   curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/reset-password \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"email":"test@eshop.com","resetToken":"<fetched_token>","newPassword":"Test@123"}'
   ```
4. Query the database:
   ```bash
   sqlite3 backend/database.sqlite "SELECT password FROM users WHERE email='test@eshop.com';"
   ```

## Expected Behavior

Per **SEC-01**:

> Mật khẩu không được lưu dưới dạng plaintext.

Specifically:

- DB state: Password must be stored as a bcrypt hash (starting with `$2`).

## Actual Behavior

- HTTP Status: `200`
- DB State: Password stored exactly as `"Test@123"` (plaintext) instead of bcrypt hashed.

## Severity Rationale

**Serious:** SEC-01 violation. Storing passwords in plaintext is a critical security vulnerability that exposes user credentials if the DB is compromised.

## Priority Rationale

**High:** Data integrity risk and major security vulnerability that must be fixed before production.

## Evidence

None — API test, no screenshot required.

> Reported by: 23127449 - Nguyễn Tấn Phát | Date: 2026-06-19
````

## Issue 2 of 6

### BUG-002 — Mobile Forgot Password page missing essential UI components

**Title:**

```text
[BUG][FR03] Mobile Forgot Password page missing essential UI components
```

**Labels:** `bug`, `severity: medium`, `FR-03`, `ui`

**Screenshot to attach:** `evidence/screenshots/FR03/BUG-002.png` — drag into body before submitting

**Body:**

````markdown
## Summary

Mobile Forgot Password page missing essential UI components when accessing step 1 and step 2 (per FR-03, FR-22)

## Bug Details

| Field          | Value                                 |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------- |
| **Bug ID**     | BUG-002                               |
| **Linked TCs** | FR03-EP-001, FR03-EP-030, FR03-EP-031 |
| **Channel**    | Mobile UI                             |
| **Severity**   | Medium                                |
| **Priority**   | High                                  |

## Environment

| Component    | Value                 |
| ------------ | --------------------- |
| OS / Device  | Windows 11            |
| Runtime Env  | Expo Go               |
| Backend      | http://localhost:3000 |
| Frontend/App | Mobile App            |
| Test Account | test@eshop.com        |

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open the EShop Mobile App in Expo Go.
2. Navigate to the Forgot Password screen (Step 1).
3. Observe the absence of a "Step Indicator" (e.g., Bước 1/2), "Quay lại đăng nhập" button, and `*` markers for required fields.
4. Enter `test@eshop.com` and proceed to Step 2.
5. Observe that the "Confirm Password" field is completely missing from the UI.

## Expected Behavior

Per **FR-03** and **FR-22**:

> Giao diện phải hiển thị chỉ báo bước (Step Indicator). Có nút **Quay lại đăng nhập**. Phải có trường **Xác nhận mật khẩu mới**.

Specifically:

- UI: Must have step indicator, back button, required markers, and confirm password field.

## Actual Behavior

- UI: Missing OTP display on screen, missing step indicator, back button, required markers, and confirm password field.

## Severity Rationale

**Medium:** Missing UI elements required by SRS. The absence of the confirm password field breaks the intended UX flow preventing users from double-checking their new password.

## Priority Rationale

**High:** Core user flow deviated heavily from the SRS requirements, directly impacting the usability of the feature.

## Evidence

_(Drag and drop the screenshot here — GitHub will auto-upload it)_

_Screenshot file: `evidence/screenshots/FR03/BUG-002.png`_

> Reported by: 23127449 - Nguyễn Tấn Phát | Date: 2026-06-19
````

## Issue 3 of 6

### BUG-003 — API generates 4-digit OTP instead of 6-digit OTP

**Title:**

```text
[BUG][FR03] API generates 4-digit OTP instead of 6-digit OTP
```

**Labels:** `bug`, `severity: serious`, `FR-03`, `api`, `security`

**Screenshot to attach:** None — API test, no screenshot required

**Body:**

````markdown
## Summary

Forgot Password API generates 4-digit OTP instead of 6-digit OTP when requesting reset (per FR-03, SEC-07)

## Bug Details

| Field          | Value                     |
| -------------- | ------------------------- |
| **Bug ID**     | BUG-003                   |
| **Linked TCs** | FR03-EP-001, FR03-BVA-009 |
| **Channel**    | API                       |
| **Severity**   | Serious                   |
| **Priority**   | High                      |

## Environment

| Component    | Value                 |
| ------------ | --------------------- |
| OS / Device  | Windows 11            |
| Runtime Env  | Node.js 24.x          |
| Backend      | http://localhost:3000 |
| Frontend/App | N/A (API-only test)   |
| Test Account | test@eshop.com        |

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Ensure the EShop backend is running at http://localhost:3000.
2. Send the following request:
   ```bash
   curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/forgot-password \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"email":"test@eshop.com"}'
   ```
3. Observe the response body and the length of the `resetToken`.

## Expected Behavior

Per **FR-03**:

> Hệ thống sinh mã OTP 6 chữ số ngẫu nhiên.

Specifically:

- HTTP 200 OK
- Response body: OTP generated must be exactly 6 digits long.

## Actual Behavior

- HTTP Status: `200`
- Response Body:
  ```json
  { "message": "Mã đặt lại mật khẩu đã được tạo", "resetToken": "1234" }
  ```

## Severity Rationale

**Serious:** FR-03 violation. A 4-digit OTP has insufficient entropy (only 10,000 combinations) and makes the system highly vulnerable to brute-force attacks.

## Priority Rationale

**High:** Security vulnerability affecting the core authentication recovery flow.

## Evidence

None — API test, no screenshot required.

> Reported by: 23127449 - Nguyễn Tấn Phát | Date: 2026-06-19
````

## Issue 4 of 6

### BUG-004 — API returns 404 instead of 400 for bad email format

**Title:**

```text
[BUG][FR03] API returns 404 instead of 400 for bad email format
```

**Labels:** `bug`, `severity: medium`, `FR-03`, `ui`, `api`

**Screenshot to attach:** `evidence/screenshots/FR03/BUG-004.png` — drag into body before submitting

**Body:**

````markdown
## Summary

Forgot password API returns HTTP 404 instead of 400 when email format is invalid or empty (per FR-03)

## Bug Details

| Field          | Value                                                                                       |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Bug ID**     | BUG-004                                                                                     |
| **Linked TCs** | FR03-EP-002, FR03-EP-003, FR03-EP-004, FR03-EP-006, FR03-EP-007, FR03-BVA-014, FR03-BVA-015 |
| **Channel**    | UI + API                                                                                    |
| **Severity**   | Medium                                                                                      |
| **Priority**   | Medium                                                                                      |

## Environment

| Component    | Value                 |
| ------------ | --------------------- |
| OS / Device  | Windows 11            |
| Runtime Env  | Node.js 24.x          |
| Backend      | http://localhost:3000 |
| Frontend/App | Mobile App / cURL     |
| Test Account | test@eshop.com        |

## Steps to Reproduce

**[UI Reproduction]:**

1. Open the EShop Mobile App in Expo Go.
2. Navigate to the Forgot Password screen.
3. Enter `invalidemail` into the Email field and submit.
4. Observe the incorrect "User not found" generic popup instead of a proper format validation message.

**[API Reproduction]:**

1. Ensure the EShop backend is running at http://localhost:3000.
2. Send the following request:
   ```bash
   curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/forgot-password \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"email":"invalidemail"}'
   ```
3. Observe the HTTP response status code.

## Expected Behavior

Per **FR-03**:

> Email phải có định dạng hợp lệ.

Specifically:

- HTTP 400 Bad Request
- UI should display proper email validation error.

## Actual Behavior

- HTTP Status: `404`
- UI: Shows generic "User not found" error instead of validating format.

## Severity Rationale

**Medium:** Wrong HTTP status and wrong UI feedback. The system incorrectly queries the DB before validating the email format, skipping standard input validation.

## Priority Rationale

**Medium:** Deviation from SRS that is visible to the user, but the request is still ultimately rejected and does not pose a security threat.

## Evidence

_(Drag and drop the screenshot here — GitHub will auto-upload it)_

_Screenshot file: `evidence/screenshots/FR03/BUG-004.png`_

> Reported by: 23127449 - Nguyễn Tấn Phát | Date: 2026-06-19
````

## Issue 5 of 6

### BUG-005 — API accepts OTP after expiration period

**Title:**

```text
[BUG][FR03] API accepts OTP after expiration period
```

**Labels:** `bug`, `severity: serious`, `FR-03`, `api`, `security`

**Screenshot to attach:** None — API test, no screenshot required

**Body:**

````markdown
## Summary

Reset Password API accepts OTP after expiration period when attempting to reset password (per SEC-07)

## Bug Details

| Field          | Value       |
| -------------- | ----------- |
| **Bug ID**     | BUG-005     |
| **Linked TCs** | FR03-EP-017 |
| **Channel**    | API         |
| **Severity**   | Serious     |
| **Priority**   | High        |

## Environment

| Component    | Value                 |
| ------------ | --------------------- |
| OS / Device  | Windows 11            |
| Runtime Env  | Node.js 24.x          |
| Backend      | http://localhost:3000 |
| Frontend/App | N/A (API-only test)   |
| Test Account | test@eshop.com        |

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Ensure the EShop backend is running at http://localhost:3000.
2. Send a request to get an OTP:
   ```bash
   curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/forgot-password \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"email":"test@eshop.com"}'
   ```
3. Wait for 60 seconds to naturally expire the OTP.
4. Send the following request to reset the password using the expired OTP:
   ```bash
   curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/reset-password \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"email":"test@eshop.com","resetToken":"<expired_otp>","newPassword":"Test@123"}'
   ```
5. Observe the HTTP response status code.

## Expected Behavior

Per **SEC-07**:

> OTP đặt lại mật khẩu phải có thời hạn.

Specifically:

- HTTP 400 or 401 Unauthorized.

## Actual Behavior

- HTTP Status: `200`
- API correctly resets password; OTP expiration is not enforced.

## Severity Rationale

**Serious:** SEC-07 violation. A lack of OTP expiration allows attackers an infinite window to brute-force or intercept the token.

## Priority Rationale

**High:** Security vulnerability affecting the authentication recovery flow.

## Evidence

None — API test, no screenshot required.

> Reported by: 23127449 - Nguyễn Tấn Phát | Date: 2026-06-19
````

## Issue 6 of 6

### BUG-006 — API accepts weak or missing new passwords

**Title:**

```text
[BUG][FR03] API accepts weak or missing new passwords
```

**Labels:** `bug`, `severity: serious`, `FR-03`, `api`

**Screenshot to attach:** None — API test, no screenshot required

**Body:**

````markdown
## Summary

Reset Password API accepts weak or missing new passwords instead of rejecting them (per FR-01)

## Bug Details

| Field          | Value                                                                                                                              |
| -------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Bug ID**     | BUG-006                                                                                                                            |
| **Linked TCs** | FR03-EP-022, FR03-EP-023, FR03-EP-024, FR03-EP-025, FR03-EP-026, FR03-EP-027, FR03-EP-028, FR03-EP-029, FR03-BVA-001, FR03-BVA-002 |
| **Channel**    | API                                                                                                                                |
| **Severity**   | Serious                                                                                                                            |
| **Priority**   | High                                                                                                                               |

## Environment

| Component    | Value                 |
| ------------ | --------------------- |
| OS / Device  | Windows 11            |
| Runtime Env  | Node.js 24.x          |
| Backend      | http://localhost:3000 |
| Frontend/App | N/A (API-only test)   |
| Test Account | test@eshop.com        |

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Ensure the EShop backend is running at http://localhost:3000.
2. Obtain a fresh OTP for `test@eshop.com` via `POST /api/forgot-password`.
3. Send the following request with a weak password (e.g. missing 8 characters):
   ```bash
   curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/reset-password \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"email":"test@eshop.com","resetToken":"<fresh_otp>","newPassword":"Te@1"}'
   ```
4. Observe the HTTP response status code.

## Expected Behavior

Per **FR-01**:

> Yêu cầu mật khẩu mạnh: Tối thiểu 8 ký tự, có ít nhất 1 chữ hoa, 1 chữ thường, 1 chữ số và 1 ký tự đặc biệt.

Specifically:

- HTTP 400 Bad Request.

## Actual Behavior

- HTTP Status: `200`
- API resets password successfully bypassing all strength validations.

## Severity Rationale

**Serious:** The backend relies purely on frontend UI validation and fails to enforce mandatory password strength rules, allowing malicious actors to set weak passwords via API.

## Priority Rationale

**High:** Core business rule completely ignored at the API layer, leading to data integrity and security risks.

## Evidence

None — API test, no screenshot required.

> Reported by: 23127449 - Nguyễn Tấn Phát | Date: 2026-06-19
````
